{"step_by_step_thinking": "The special visceral afferent (SVA) fibers of the facial nerve are responsible for sensory information, particularly taste from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. Reviewing the documents, Document [18] states that the SVA component of the facial nerve carries taste information from the anterior two-thirds of the tongue and terminates at the ipsilateral nucleus of the solitary tract in the rostral medulla. Therefore, the special visceral afferent fibers of the facial nerve are located in the nucleus of tractus solitarius.", "answer_choice": "C"}
